channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Obama urges Black men to show up for Harris as he campaigns in critical Pennsylvania
item.description: PITTSBURGH (AP) — Former President Barack Obama gave a blistering critique of his White House successor Donald Trump and urged Black men to show up for Kamala Harris as he opened a swing-state tour for the Democratic ticket.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Residents repair their homes and clean up after Hurricane Milton tore through Florida
item.description: ST. PETERSBURG, Fla. (AP) — Florida residents repaired damage from Hurricane Milton and cleaned up debris Friday after the storm smashed through coastal communities and tore homes to pieces, flooded streets and spawned a barrage of deadly tornadoes.
item.dc:creator: JULIO CORTEZ, KATE PAYNE and HAVEN DALEY, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Millions remain in the dark after Hurricane Milton
item.description: Florida residents repaired damage from Hurricane Milton and cleaned up debris Friday after the storm smashed through coastal communities and tore homes to pieces, flooded streets and spawned a barrage of deadly tornadoes.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Biden suggests Trump 'get a life' after false hurricane claims
item.description: “Mr. President Trump, former President Trump, get a life man, help these people,” Biden said in remarks on Hurricane Milton from the White House.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KRON4

item.title: Tampa sees 5x the usual October rainfall due to Hurricane Milton
item.description: Hurricane Milton's landfall as a Category 3 hurricane didn't just bring devastating storm surge and extremely dangerous wind speeds, it also left Tampa Bay soaked with historic rainfall.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Rodriguez, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Internet Archive data breach exposes more than 31 million user accounts: reports
item.description: The Internet Archive, a popular digital library known for its Wayback Machine, was hacked and suffered a breach that reportedly exposed the data of 31 million user accounts.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Jackson, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 19 people in Missouri, Kansas indicted for meth and fentanyl trafficking ring
item.description: Prosecutors said law enforcement seized approximately 100 pounds of meth and two pounds of fentanyl pills from a drug car.
item.dc:creator: Kevin S. Held, NewsNation

item.title: Military sends resources to Florida in wake of Hurricane Milton while Helene recovery efforts continue
item.description: The U.S. military is flooding resources into storm-ravaged Florida while also continuing to tend to those affected in North Carolina by Hurricane Helene.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Milton by the numbers: At least 5 dead, at least 12 tornadoes, 3.4M without power
item.description: Hurricane Milton's explosive formation stirred high anxiety for Florida less than two weeks after Helene raked the state and much of the Southeast. On Thursday, authorities were still assessing the damage, even as Gov. Ron DeSantis said it fell short of “the worst-case scenario.
item.dc:creator: MARY KATHERINE WILDEMAN and CHRISTOPHER L. KELLER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Will we see northern lights, power grid impacts amid 'severe' geomagnetic storm?
item.description: Earth is being hit by a strong solar radiation storm event that will be followed by a geomagnetic storm, space weather officials say.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, NewsNation

item.title: Although Milton has moved on, at least 8 dead and millions in the dark
item.description: Hurricane Milton barreled into the Atlantic Ocean on Thursday after plowing across Florida, where it knocked out power to more than 3 million customers and whipped up a barrage of tornadoes.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Police rescue 15 people from Tampa home after Milton knocks tree on house
item.description: Officers with the Tampa Police Department were sheltering a few miles when they were alerted to the incident via a 911 call.
item.dc:creator: Juan Cisneros, KRON4

item.title: COVID may increase risk of heart attack, death years after infection: study
item.description: COVID-19 could increase the risk of major cardiac events, like strokes and heart attacks, or death from any cause, according to a new study.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, ABC 4

item.title: Jenna Fischer of 'The Office' reveals breast cancer diagnosis, recovery
item.description: Jenna Fischer, the actress well-known for her role as Pam on "The Office," revealed her diagnosis and recovery in a post to social media.
item.dc:creator: Joey Schneider, KTLA 5

item.title: More than 2 million without power as Hurricane Milton slams Florida, causes deaths and flooding
item.description: TAMPA, Fla. (AP) — Hurricane Milton plowed into Florida as a Category 3 storm Wednesday, bringing misery to a coast still ravaged by Helene, pounding cities with winds of over 100 mph (160 kph) after producing a barrage of tornadoes, but sparing Tampa a direct hit.
item.dc:creator: TERRY SPENCER and KATE PAYNE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Watch: Massive 28-foot wave captured inside Hurricane Milton
item.description: Drone footage captured a monstrous wave inside Hurricane Milton as the powerful storm passed through the Gulf of Mexico on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, KRON4

item.title: Lawyers: Sean 'Diddy' Combs seeks trial next April or May on sex trafficking charges
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Sean ‘Diddy’ Combs wants to go to trial on sex trafficking charges next spring, lawyers for the jailed hip-hop mogul told a judge on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Hurricane Milton slams Florida as a Category 3 storm; leaving millions in the dark
item.description: Hurricane Milton made landfall Wednesday along Florida’s Gulf Coast as a Category 3 storm, bringing powerful winds, deadly storm surge and potential flooding to much of the state. Milton drew fuel from exceedingly warm Gulf of Mexico waters, twice reaching Category 5 status.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Why do people fill their bathtubs with water before a hurricane?
item.description: If you live in a hurricane-prone area, it may be common practice for you. If you live well outside that region, you may be confused by the move.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, WGN 9

item.title: What makes a storm a hurricane? The dangers across 5 categories
item.description: Severe weather can target any part of the globe, but each year the Atlantic hurricane season brings heavy rain, strong wind, storm surge and sometimes catastrophic devastation to locations from the Caribbean islands and Central America all the way up through the United States and into Canada.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: West Virginia woman met her husband after getting donor lungs from his late sister
item.description: A woman from West Virginia got a new chance at life and found the love of her life thanks to organ donation.
item.dc:creator: Sam Kirk, WGN 9

item.title: States sue TikTok, alleging platform is addictive, harms kids' mental health
item.description: More than a dozen states and the District of Columbia sued TikTok on Tuesday, alleging the platform exploits and harms young users while "deceiving" the public about these dangers.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, ABC 4

item.title: Is it safe to be outside in Hurricane Milton's eye? Here's what experts say
item.description: The eye of a hurricane is usually calm compared to the storm swirling around it — but is it safe to be outside as the eye passes over you?
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Rodriguez, ABC 4

item.title: California 'Dreamer' to attend DACA hearing that could determine program's fate
item.description: "The tone was never been let’s compromise, let’s meet in the middle, let’s help these kids, let’s help this talent, so they can further help this country, it has always been you are unlawful, you are illegal, you are an alien.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, Fox 5

item.title: Raccoon sparks chaos in stands during Broncos game against Raiders
item.description: Football fans packed Empower Field in Denver on Sunday to watch the Broncos take on the Las Vegas Raiders, but they weren’t the only ones in attendance.
item.dc:creator: Maddie Rhodes, ABC 4

item.title: Fearful residents flee Tampa Bay region as Hurricane Milton takes aim at Florida coast
item.description: Fearful Florida residents streamed out of the Tampa Bay region Tuesday ahead of what could be a once-in-a-century direct hit from Hurricane Milton.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Hurricane Milton is a Category 5. Florida orders evacuations and scrambles to clear Helene's debris
item.description: BELLEAIR BEACH, Fla. (AP) — Florida’s storm-battered Gulf Coast raced against a Category 5 hurricane Monday as workers sprinted to pick up debris left over from Helene two weeks ago and highways were clogged with people fleeing ahead of the storm.
item.dc:creator: HAVEN DALEY and MIKE SCHNEIDER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: The Latest: Hurricane Milton strengthens into a Category 5 as Florida prepares for evacuations
item.description: Milton rapidly strengthened in the Gulf of Mexico on Monday, becoming a Category 5 storm on a path toward Florida.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: What to know as Florida residents begin cleaning up after Hurricane Milton
item.description: Cleanup from Hurricane Milton is underway as residents are returning home after the storm barreled across Florida.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: These cities are the 'safest' in America, new study says
item.description: The quality of schools, traffic and entertainment offerings are among the variety of factors you consider before moving to a new city. Another is safety.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: Costco gold bars are selling out as prices for the precious metal break records: survey
item.description: Move over, gas and hot dogs. Costco shoppers are snatching up the store's 1-ounce gold bars despite the market's lofty price for the precious metal.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, WGN 9

item.title: Chicago mayor names new school board after entire panel resigns amid a fight over district control
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Chicago Mayor Brandon Johnson named members of a new school board on Monday, batting away criticism of the move days after all seven members resigned amid an escalating fight over control of the public schools in the nation’s third-largest city.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIA TAREEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Tennessee nurse and his dog died trying to save a man from floods driven by Helene
item.description: “I’ve never had anyone risk their life for me,” David Boutin, the man Boone McCrary had set out to rescue said. “From what I hear that was the way he always been. He’s my guardian angel, that’s for sure.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Groups suspend use of 'suicide capsule' pending criminal probe of US woman's death
item.description: Swiss police arrested several people following the death of an unidentified 64-year-old woman from the U.S. Midwest who on Sept. 23 became the first person to use the device.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Elon Musk makes appearance at Trump rally casting election in dire terms
item.description: (AP) - Billionaire tech executive Elon Musk cast the upcoming presidential election in dire terms during a Saturday appearance with Donald Trump, calling the Republican presidential nominee the only candidate “to preserve democracy in America.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Hurricane Helene aftermath poses election hurdles in critical battlegrounds
item.description: Hurricane Helene has thrown up new hurdles for voters and election officials alike in Georgia and North Carolina, threatening disruptions to the voting process in two of the most critical battleground states.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, WGN 9

item.title: Keanu Reeves spins out at Indianapolis Motor Speedway in pro auto racing debut
item.description: Hollywood star Keanu Reeves made his professional auto racing debut on Saturday in an event in which “The Matrix” star spun out at famed Indianapolis Motor Speedway.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9
